item.title: Thoughts on OpenAI acquiring Astral and uv/ruff/ty 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI is acquiring Astral, the company behind key Python tools uv, ruff, and ty. Astral's team will join OpenAI's Codex team, with both companies committing to continue supporting these open-source projects. The acquisition aims to enhance Codex's capabilities in software development, leveraging Astral's engineering expertise. Concerns include potential impacts on the Python ecosystem, competitive dynamics with Anthropic's acquisition of Bun, and the forking strategy as a fallback if maintenance falters. The deal highlights the growing importance of coding agents and infrastructure in AI-driven development.
标签: OpenAI, Astral, Python, uv, ruff, ty, acquisition, open-source, Codex, coding-agents

item.title: ★ AppleScript: ‘Save MarsEdit Document to Text File’ 摘要
item.description:
The author wrote an AppleScript to save MarsEdit blog drafts as text files, addressing a long-standing workflow issue. The script adds a 'Save as Text File...' feature via FastScripts, prompting for a filename and location, and exports document fields like Title, Tags, and Excerpt into a formatted text file. This helped clean up 29 old drafts, moving them from MarsEdit's local library to Dropbox for better organization and future editing in BBEdit.
标签: AppleScript, MarsEdit, blogging, workflow, automation, Mac, text files, productivity, FastScripts, BBEdit

item.title: Hacker News Discussion on Shubham Bose’s ‘The 49MB Web Page’ 摘要
item.description:
The author argues that supporting JavaScript in web browsers was a fundamental mistake, as it transformed web pages from documents into embedded programs. This shift enabled issues like massive 49 MB pages, pervasive surveillance tracking, and intrusive user experiences (e.g., popovers that obstruct reading), which publishers would never allow in print editions. JavaScript's invisibility makes such problematic practices seem acceptable, contrasting with the visible nature of text and media.
标签: web, JavaScript, browsers, privacy, surveillance, web development, user experience, controversial opinions, technology critique, publishing

item.title: Google’s New Sideloading Restrictions for Android Include a 24-Hour Waiting Period 摘要
item.description:
Google is implementing new sideloading restrictions on Android that will make installing apps outside the Play Store significantly more difficult. The new rules include a mandatory 24-hour waiting period before users can install apps from unverified developers, which Google describes as a "high-friction process." This follows previous warnings from Google executives about tightening sideloading controls. The changes are part of Google's broader Android developer verification program aimed at increasing security, though critics argue they reduce platform openness. Notably, Epic Games CEO Tim Sweeney has agreed to deem Google's Android practices "procompetitive" until 2032 as part of a legal settlement.
标签: Android, Google, sideloading, mobile, app stores, security, Play Store, developer verification, platform restrictions, Epic Games

item.title: The Day Mark Simonson Discovered Type Design 摘要
item.description:
Mark Simonson recounts discovering a copy of U&lc magazine in a graphics classroom, published by ITC (International Typeface Corporation) with design and editing by the legendary Herb Lubalin. He was struck by the beautiful typography and design, describing it as a "motherlode" for an aspiring typophile. This experience inspired him to decide that he wanted to design typefaces someday.
标签: typography, typeface design, Herb Lubalin, U&lc magazine, ITC, graphic design, inspiration, Mark Simonson, design history, typophile

item.title: ‘Everyone but Trump Understands What He’s Done’ 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses the geopolitical and economic consequences of former President Donald Trump's foreign policy actions, particularly regarding U.S. alliances and the Iran conflict. It highlights Trump's history of mocking and insulting European allies, undermining NATO support, and ridiculing countries like Canada and the U.K., which has strained international relations. Simultaneously, the Iran war has disrupted global oil supplies, with prices potentially soaring from around $60 to $150–200 per barrel, leading to predictions of sustained high gasoline costs in the U.S. and broader economic turmoil. The piece critiques these developments as a self-inflicted crisis, using the phrase 'hoist with his own petard' to describe the situation.
标签: geopolitics, foreign policy, oil prices, international relations, economic impact, Trump administration, Iran conflict, NATO, energy crisis, global economy

item.title: Actual Headline in the Actual New York Times: ‘Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Leader’ 摘要
item.description:
In a meeting with Japan's leader, former President Trump was asked why Japan and other allies weren't notified in advance of a U.S.-Israeli assault on Iran. He responded by referencing the surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, saying, 'We didn't tell anybody about it because we wanted surprise. Who knows better about surprise than Japan, OK? Why didn't you tell me about Pearl Harbor, OK? Right?' The remark drew laughter from officials and journalists present. The article notes this as an example of Trump's increasingly erratic behavior amid concerns about his mental state.
标签: politics, diplomacy, Trump, Japan, Iran, Pearl Harbor, surprise attack, international relations, controversy, humor

item.title: StopTheMadness Pro and StopTheScript Extensions for Safari 摘要
item.description:
This article discusses browser extensions that enhance user experience by blocking unwanted web content. StopTheMadness Pro for Safari prevents autoplaying videos and hides 'Sign in with Google' prompts, while StopTheScript disables JavaScript on selected sites to improve readability, such as on The Guardian. For Chrome/Chromium, Quick JavaScript Switcher offers a free toolbar toggle for JavaScript, with a donation option. The author notes that disabling JavaScript often removes undesirable elements without loss of essential functionality, and humorously mentions OnlyAds, an extension that hides everything except ads. These tools are presented as solutions to common web frustrations.
标签: browser extensions, Safari, Chrome, JavaScript, user experience, ad blocking, web development, privacy, productivity, web design
